children.name: RegisterPatient
children.purpose: Register a new patient in the system
children.behavior: Extract patient details from input, validate uniqueness, store in patients data store, and return success or error
postconditions: new patient record created in patients data store

call_inputs.name: input
call_inputs.description: JSON with registration details (name, contact, etc.)

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Check uniqueness and store new patient record

outputs.name: registration_result
outputs.description: JSON with success status, patient ID, and message

global_vars.description: Check uniqueness and store new patient record

data_operations.description: Check uniqueness and store new patient record

constraints.description: Patient ID must be unique across all records

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid input, a new patient record is created and a success response is returned
Given duplicate patient data, an error response is returned and no record is created

children.name: BookAppointment
children.purpose: Book an appointment for a registered patient
children.behavior: Extract patient ID and appointment details from input, verify patient exists, check slot availability, store appointment, and return confirmation
postconditions: new appointment record created in appointments data store

call_inputs.name: input
call_inputs.description: JSON with patient ID, date, time, doctor, etc.

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Check slot availability and store new appointment

outputs.name: booking_result
outputs.description: JSON with success status, appointment ID, and message

global_vars.description: Check slot availability and store new appointment

data_operations.description: Check slot availability and store new appointment

constraints.description: Appointment slot must be available (no double-booking)

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid input and available slot, a new appointment is created and a success response is returned
Given unavailable slot, an error response is returned and no appointment is created
Given unregistered patient, an error response is returned

children.name: GetRecords
children.purpose: Retrieve medical records for a patient
children.behavior: Extract patient ID from input, verify patient exists, query appointments data store for all records, and return them

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Query all records for the patient

outputs.name: records_result
outputs.description: JSON with success status, list of records, and message

global_vars.description: Query all records for the patient

data_operations.description: Query all records for the patient

acceptance_criteria.description: Given registered patient ID, all records are returned successfully
Given unregistered patient ID, an error response is returned

children.name: UpdateProfile
children.behavior: Extract patient ID and updated fields from input, verify patient exists, update patients data store, and return confirmation
postconditions: patient profile updated in patients data store

call_inputs.name: input
call_inputs.description: JSON with patient ID and fields to update

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Verify patient exists and update profile

outputs.name: update_result
outputs.description: JSON with success status and message

global_vars.description: Verify patient exists and update profile

data_operations.description: Verify patient exists and update profile

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid input and registered patient, profile is updated and success response is returned
Given unregistered patient, an error response is returned

children.name: ParseCommand
children.purpose: Parse the input JSON to determine which command to execute
children.behavior: Extract the 'command' field from input JSON and return it along with the rest of the data
guarantees: command is one of the valid commands

outputs.name: data
outputs.description: Remaining JSON data for the command

constraints.description: Command must be one of: register, book, records, update

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid JSON with command field, command and data are extracted correctly
Given missing command field, an error is returned

children.name: ValidateInput
children.purpose: Validate the input JSON structure and required fields
children.behavior: Check that input has required fields based on command, return validation result

outputs.name: validation_result
outputs.description: JSON with boolean valid flag and optional error message

constraints.description: Validation rules must be deterministic and consistent

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid input, validation returns valid=true
Given invalid input, validation returns valid=false with error message

children.name: FormatResponse
children.purpose: Format the output JSON with success, data, and message
children.behavior: Take the result from a child operation and wrap it in a standard response format

call_inputs.name: result
call_inputs.description: Result from a child operation (success, data, message)

constraints.description: Response format must be consistent across all operations

acceptance_criteria.description: Given a result dict, output is formatted with success, data, and message fields

governance_notes: All governance and resource fields derived from Stage 2 internal_leaf_accesses. Children with no internal_leaf_accesses (ParseCommand, ValidateInput, FormatResponse) have empty global_vars and data_operations. For children with data store accesses, global_vars and data_operations are synchronized with the same resources and operations. Requested_capabilities follow the pattern 'resource.operation'. Constraints and acceptance criteria are derived from preconditions, postconditions, and guarantees. Node types: pure_function for read-only or transform operations, atomic_operation for write operations.

resource_audit.signature_preservation_detail: All 7 child signatures from Stage 2 are preserved exactly. No changes to call_inputs, outputs, or signature strings.
resource_audit.internal_leaf_mapping_detail: Each internal_leaf_accesses entry maps directly to a global_vars entry with the same resource and op. For example, RegisterPatient's internal_leaf_accesses [patients read_write] maps to global_vars [patients read_write] and data_operations [patients read_write]. BookAppointment's two accesses map to two global_vars entries.
resource_audit.global_vars_data_operations_sync_detail: For every child with internal_leaf_accesses, global_vars and data_operations contain identical entries. Children without internal_leaf_accesses have empty arrays for both. No exceptions needed.
resource_audit.coverage_detail: All resources from internal_leaf_accesses are covered: patients (read, read_write) and appointments (read, read_write). No resources are missing from governance fields.
